title: muehlenbeckia monticola
intro: Muehlenbeckia monticola, also known as the climbing lily vine or petioled five hearts, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Muehlenbeckia monticola are often used in traditional medicine, including infusions, tinctures, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
